name: Word
statement: Word (α : Type*) := List α
description: Core representation of a finite word over an alphabet α as a list of symbols.

name: Word.empty
statement: Word.empty : Word α := []
description: The empty word, denoted by ε in literature.

name: Word.length
statement: Word.length (w : Word α) : ℕ := List.length w
description: Length of a word, delegating to Mathlib's List.length.

name: Word.letter
statement: def Word.letter (a : α) : Word α := [a]
description: Single-letter word.

name: Word.concat
statement: Word.concat (u v : Word α) : Word α := u ++ v
description: Concatenation of two words u and v. Denoted by the infix notation ⬝.

name: Word.length_concat
statement: lemma Word.length_concat (u v : Word α) : (u ⬝ v).length = u.length + v.length
description: The length of the concatenation of two words is the sum of their lengths.

name: Word.concat_empty_right
statement: lemma Word.concat_empty_right (w : Word α) : w ⬝ Word.empty = w
description: Concatenating the empty word on the right has no effect.

name: Word.concat_empty_left
statement: lemma Word.concat_empty_left (w : Word α) : Word.empty ⬝ w = w
description: Concatenating the empty word on the left has no effect.

name: Word.concat_assoc
statement: lemma Word.concat_assoc (u v w : Word α) : (u ⬝ v) ⬝ w = u ⬝ (v ⬝ w)
description: Word concatenation is associative.

name: Monoid
statement: instance : Monoid (Word α)
description: Words form a monoid under concatenation.

name: Word.mul_eq_concat
statement: lemma Word.mul_eq_concat (u v : Word α) : u * v = u ⬝ v
description: Multiplication in the Word monoid is equivalent to concatenation.

name: Word.one_eq_empty
statement: lemma Word.one_eq_empty : (1 : Word α) = Word.empty
description: The unit element 1 in the monoid is the empty word.

name: Word.pow_zero
statement: lemma Word.pow_zero (w : Word α) : w ^ 0 = Word.empty
description: Any word raised to power 0 is the empty word.

name: Word.pow_succ
statement: lemma Word.pow_succ (w : Word α) (n : ℕ) : w ^ (n + 1) = w ^ n ⬝ w
description: Inductive step for word power definition.

name: IsPrefix
statement: def IsPrefix (u w : Word α) : Prop := List.IsPrefix u w
description: Predicate stating that u is a prefix of w. Denoted by u ≤ₚ w.

name: IsPrefix.def
statement: lemma IsPrefix.def (u w : Word α) : (u ≤ₚ w) ↔ ∃ v, w = u ++ v
description: Definition of prefix using list append.

name: IsPrefix.length_le
statement: lemma IsPrefix.length_le {u w : Word α} (h : u ≤ₚ w) : u.length ≤ w.length
description: A prefix of a word has length less than or equal to the word's length.

name: IsSuffix
statement: def IsSuffix (u w : Word α) : Prop := List.IsSuffix u w
description: Predicate stating that u is a suffix of w. Denoted by u ≤ₛ w.

name: IsSuffix.def
statement: lemma IsSuffix.def (u w : Word α) : (u ≤ₛ w) ↔ ∃ v, w = v ++ u
description: Definition of suffix using list append.

name: IsFactor
statement: def IsFactor (u w : Word α) : Prop := ∃ x y : Word α, w = x ++ u ++ y
description: Predicate stating that u is a contiguous factor of w. Denoted by u ≤f w.

name: IsProperPrefix
statement: def IsProperPrefix (u w : Word α) : Prop := u ≤ₚ w ∧ u ≠ w
description: Predicate stating that u is a prefix of w and is not equal to w. Denoted by u <ₚ w.

name: IsProperPrefix.def
statement: lemma IsProperPrefix.def (u w : Word α) : (u <ₚ w) ↔ ∃ v, w = u ++ v ∧ v ≠ []
description: Definition of proper prefix using non-empty remainder.

name: IsProperSuffix
statement: def IsProperSuffix (u w : Word α) : Prop := u ≤ₛ w ∧ u ≠ w
description: Predicate stating that u is a suffix of w and is not equal to w. Denoted by u <ₛ w.

name: IsProperSuffix.iff
statement: lemma IsProperSuffix.iff (u w : Word α) : (u <ₛ w) ↔ ∃ v, w = v ++ u ∧ v ≠ []
description: Definition of proper suffix using non-empty remainder.

name: IsBorder
statement: def IsBorder (b w : Word α) : Prop := b <ₚ w ∧ b <ₛ w
description: A border of w is a word that is simultaneously a proper prefix and a proper suffix of w.

name: IsPrimitive
statement: def IsPrimitive (w : Word α) : Prop := w ≠ [] ∧ ¬ ∃ (u : Word α) (k : ℕ), k ≥ 2 ∧ w = u ^ k
description: A word is primitive if it is non-empty and cannot be expressed as a power of another word with exponent k ≥ 2.

name: IsConjugate
statement: def IsConjugate (u v : Word α) : Prop := ∃ x y : Word α, u = x ⬝ y ∧ v = y ⬝ x
description: Two words are conjugate if one is a cyclic shift of the other. Denoted by u ~ v.

name: applyMorphism
statement: def applyMorphism (f : α → Word β) (w : Word α) : Word β := w.flatMap f
description: Applies a morphism f (defined by letter mappings) to a word w by concatenating the images of its letters.

name: IsSquare
statement: def IsSquare (s : Word α) : Prop := ∃ u : Word α, u ≠ [] ∧ s = u ++ u
description: Predicate stating that a word is a square (of the form uu for some non-empty u).
